title: Zero-divisor prime labelings family campaign
family_name: Prime labelings of zero-divisor graph families
theorem_slice_target: Paired support-template reduction theorems for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2).
fallback_target: A smallest-template-failure obstruction if the current arithmetic reservoir pattern breaks first at p = 11 or p = 13.
current_blocker: The support reductions are visible but not yet formalized as explicit theorem slices, and the arithmetic reservoir arguments past the solved cluster are still unproved.
theorem_slice_hint: Start with a support-template theorem for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2), then pressure-test p = 11 as the next feeder.
why_now: The repo already has a cluster of Lean-backed exact instances with repeated support-class decompositions and reusable label roles.

recommended_feeders.title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_11 × Z_25) prime?
recommended_feeders.question: Does the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_11 × Z_25) admit a prime labeling?
recommended_feeders.canonical_statement: Determine whether Γ(Z_11 × Z_25) is prime.
recommended_feeders.intended_statement: The exact feeder target is the instance Γ(Z_11 × Z_25), used to pressure-test the current Γ(Z_p × Z_25) support-template theorem slice at the first decisive unsolved odd prime.
recommended_feeders.named_conjecture: Prime labelings of zero-divisor graph families
recommended_feeders.attack_style: support-template feeder for a publication campaign
recommended_feeders.theorem_slice_hint: Pressure-test the Γ(Z_p × Z_25) support-template reduction at p = 11.
recommended_feeders.why_still_appears_open: The repo has Lean-backed exacts at p = 3,5,7 but no preserved exact result at p = 11.
recommended_feeders.why_this_could_be_publishable: Success or failure at p = 11 directly sharpens the current theorem-slice campaign rather than adding another isolated example.

recommended_feeders.title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_11 × Z_11 × Z_2) prime?
recommended_feeders.question: Does the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_11 × Z_11 × Z_2) admit a prime labeling?
recommended_feeders.canonical_statement: Determine whether Γ(Z_11 × Z_11 × Z_2) is prime.
recommended_feeders.intended_statement: The exact feeder target is the instance Γ(Z_11 × Z_11 × Z_2), used to pressure-test the current Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) support-template theorem slice at the first decisive unsolved odd prime.
recommended_feeders.named_conjecture: Prime labelings of zero-divisor graph families
recommended_feeders.attack_style: support-template feeder for a publication campaign
recommended_feeders.theorem_slice_hint: Pressure-test the Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) support-template reduction at p = 11.
recommended_feeders.why_still_appears_open: The repo has Lean-backed exacts at p = 5,7 but no preserved exact result at p = 11.
recommended_feeders.why_this_could_be_publishable: This is the smallest clean discriminator for whether the current six-class template is really scalable.

recommended_feeders.title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) prime?
recommended_feeders.question: Does the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) admit a prime labeling?
recommended_feeders.canonical_statement: Determine whether Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) is prime.
recommended_feeders.intended_statement: Use Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) only after p = 11 to test whether the current Γ(Z_p × Z_25) template still has arithmetic room when the feeder becomes tight.
recommended_feeders.named_conjecture: Prime labelings of zero-divisor graph families
recommended_feeders.attack_style: tightness feeder for a publication campaign
recommended_feeders.theorem_slice_hint: Use only after p = 11 to test the edge of the arithmetic reservoir.
recommended_feeders.why_still_appears_open: No exact result for p = 13 is currently preserved in the repo.
recommended_feeders.why_this_could_be_publishable: It distinguishes genuine theorem-slice scalability from a fragile small-prime phenomenon.

title: CNBC quintic nonexistence family campaign
family_name: Closed-neighborhood balanced colorings of quintic circulants
theorem_slice_target: A two-route Fourier/periodicity nonexistence theorem for selected quintic circulants.
fallback_target: A structural obstruction package with exact frontier applications C16, C20, and C24 if the unified criterion theorem is not yet clean.
current_blocker: The exact counterexamples still need to be packaged as one reusable criterion theorem, especially the opposite-pair reduction in the C20 line.
theorem_slice_hint: Package the verified exact nonexistence proofs as a Fourier and periodicity criterion for quintic circulants before hunting more isolated tuples.
why_now: The repo already contains three verified exact nonexistence arguments with a common circulant-operator backbone.

recommended_feeders.title: Is the quintic circulant C_28(4,5,14) a CNBC graph?
recommended_feeders.question: Does the quintic circulant C_28(4,5,14) admit a closed-neighborhood balanced coloring?
recommended_feeders.intended_statement: Use the exact instance C_28(4,5,14) as the next discriminator for the Fourier/periodicity campaign extending the C16 and C24 lines.
recommended_feeders.named_conjecture: Closed-neighborhood balanced colorings of quintic circulants
recommended_feeders.theorem_slice_hint: Test whether the same spectral obstruction persists on the (4,5,n/2) line at n = 28.
recommended_feeders.why_still_appears_open: The repo preserves exact nonexistence results at n = 16 and 24 on this line, but no exact result at n = 28.
recommended_feeders.why_this_could_be_publishable: It sharpens the current criterion theorem by testing whether the same low-order kernel or invertibility mechanism extends one more step.

recommended_feeders.title: Is the quintic circulant C_28(2,3,14) a CNBC graph?
recommended_feeders.question: Does the quintic circulant C_28(2,3,14) admit a closed-neighborhood balanced coloring?
recommended_feeders.intended_statement: Use the exact instance C_28(2,3,14) as the next discriminator for the opposite-pair and periodicity campaign extending the C20 line.
recommended_feeders.named_conjecture: Closed-neighborhood balanced colorings of quintic circulants
recommended_feeders.theorem_slice_hint: Test whether the same opposite-pair reduction route persists on the (2,3,n/2) line at n = 28.
recommended_feeders.why_still_appears_open: The repo preserves an exact nonexistence result at n = 20 on this line, but no exact result at n = 28.
recommended_feeders.why_this_could_be_publishable: It cleanly distinguishes a one-off reduction from a reusable family obstruction.

title: DSRG obstruction reserve campaign
family_name: Directed strongly regular graph obstruction packages
theorem_slice_target: Harvest reusable quotient and obstruction lemmas only; do not open a live theorem campaign yet.
fallback_target: Keep reserve notes until one dsrg vein stabilizes into a real family obstruction.
current_blocker: The obstruction packages are still diffuse and several promising lines have failed faithfulness or skepticism checks.
theorem_slice_hint: Hold in reserve unless one dsrg vein develops a clean family obstruction rather than another isolated local package.
why_now: The ledger contains many exact local obstruction packages, but theorem-level closure is still weaker than the top two campaigns.
